audit_scope: First slice of Intervention A: identify the biggest per-callsite MlxArray allocator in the Gemma 4 forward path, fix one concrete instance, measure impact.
method: Stack-walked the AX sample profile to attribute mlx::core::array constructor samples to immediate-child operations within ax_engine_mlx::model::layer_forward_with_turboquant_context.
Identified `gelu_approx` as the largest non-matmul allocator (94 samples direct + transitive via mk_scalar pattern).
Implemented scalar caching in `mlx-sys::ops::cached_scalar` so `gelu`/`gelu_approx` reuse the (value, dtype) pair across all calls.
Re-ran sample profile under identical workload, compared before vs after.

hot_callsite_breakdown_before_fix.scope: Immediate-child function attribution within layer_forward_with_turboquant_context, 10s sample window, 40 iterations × 32 decode tokens

fix_landed.scope: Cache scalar constants in `gelu` / `gelu_approx` so the 4 constants per call (sqrt(2/π), 0.044715, 1.0, 0.5) are materialised once per (value, dtype) pair process-wide, not per call.
files_changed: crates/mlx-sys/src/ops.rs (new `cached_scalar(value, dtype) -> MlxArray`; refactor gelu/gelu_approx mk_scalar)
fix_landed.expected_savings_per_forward_pass: Gemma 4 has ~64 gelu_approx calls per forward (32 layers × 2 sites — FFN SwiGLU + per_layer_input gating). Each previously built 4 from_raw_data + 4 astype + intermediate ops = 8 transient MlxArray instances. Theoretical saving: ~500 MlxArrays per forward pass (cache hits after first call).
fix_landed.tests: Existing 310 ax-engine-mlx unit tests pass; clippy clean.

honest_interpretation: The scalar cache fix is technically correct and removes a real source of redundant allocation, but the per-call constant reconstruction was happening on the CPU side fast enough that sample(1) at 1ms granularity does not detect it. The 5.7% drop in `mlx::core::array` constructor samples is within the noise of consecutive sample runs (we observed ~3-5% drift between identical re-runs prior to this fix). Decode tok/s is unchanged. The scalar cache stays — it's a correct optimisation with zero downside — but it does NOT close the AX-vs-mlx_lm allocation gap.
what_this_rules_out: Cached-constant reconstruction is NOT the primary contributor to AX's 2.26× array-allocation ratio versus mlx_lm.
Per-call activation-function overhead (gelu_approx, silu) is NOT in the actionable hot path.
The 1.67× gpu::eval ratio (the only barrier-related signal) does not move when allocation patterns change — confirming eval barriers aren't the lever (already concluded in eval_barrier_audit.v1).

remaining_intervention_A_candidates.candidate: Audit `fast` module (rope + scaled_dot_product_attention) for allocation amplification
remaining_intervention_A_candidates.size: 124 samples in layer_forward, largest single child
remaining_intervention_A_candidates.feasibility: Medium — mlx-sys::fast wrappers may have a similar mk_scalar-style pattern; same fix recipe would apply. But the mlx-c bindings themselves may be the source of intermediate arrays, in which case AX cannot fix from outside.

remaining_intervention_A_candidates.size: 23 samples per layer × 32 layers = 736 forward-path samples
remaining_intervention_A_candidates.feasibility: Medium — this is AX-owned code in kv_cache.rs. Likely allocates intermediate MlxArrays for sliding-window trimming.

remaining_intervention_A_candidates.candidate: Compare mlx_lm gemma3n.py forward against ax model.rs forward op-by-op
remaining_intervention_A_candidates.feasibility: High effort, uncertain payoff. mlx_lm's nn.Module composes through Python which may have similar wrapping overhead — the 2.26× ratio may reflect Rust wrapper structural overhead that no per-callsite fix addresses.

remaining_intervention_A_candidates.candidate: Stop investing in CPU-side allocation reduction; the gap may be in the Rust ↔ mlx-c FFI binding cost, not in AX code.
remaining_intervention_A_candidates.feasibility: If true, the actionable lever is upstream mlx-c API improvement or a different optimisation entirely (KV scheduler revamp, larger-model evidence).

decision_gate_status: First slice of Intervention A complete. Scalar cache shipped (correct, no regression, no measurable speedup). Remaining candidates require either more invasive code changes (option 2 — kv_cache audit, 3-5 days) or pivot to a different workstream (option 4 — accept that AX's 2× allocation ratio may be structural).
next_step_recommendation: STOP this slice. The cheapest hypothesis (scalar reconstruction) was tested and failed to deliver measurable improvement. Continuing Intervention A in the same vein (per-callsite micro-optimisations) has diminishing returns based on the sample data. Pivot decision needed: (a) deeper kv_cache audit, (b) FFI-layer investigation, or (c) move attention to a different workstream entirely. ADR 0024 D2 still requires evidence before any change — this artifact IS that evidence for the negative-result direction.
